label: 341_seasonal_seasonal adjustment_weather_alternative seasonal
description: The Bureau of Labor Statistics uses various methods to adjust employment data for seasonal effects. Economists at the Federal Reserve Banks of Atlanta, San Francisco, and New York propose alternative approaches, providing deeper insights into job growth trends.

earliest_articles.title: Cash Assets of Foreign Banks: An Example of Seasonal Adjustment Gone Awry
earliest_articles.excerpt: Adam Copeland, Todd Keister, and Parinitha Sastry*Federal ReserveStatistical Release H.8provides aggregate data on the assets and liabilities of commercial banks in the United States. newest_articles.title: A Cold Stop: Temperature, Unemployment and Joblessness Dynamics
newest_articles.excerpt: A Cold Stop: Temperature, Unemployment and Joblessness Dynamics Working Paper 34487 DOI 10.3386/w34487 Issue Date We provide new evidence that short-run temperature shocks affect unemployment dynamics. newest_articles.title: Estimating National Weather Effects from the Ground Up
newest_articles.excerpt: Understanding the effects of weather on macroeconomic data is critically important, but it is hampered by limited time series observations. Utilizing geographically granular panel data leverages greater observations but introduces a “missing intercept” problem: “global” (e.g., nationwide spillovers and GE) effects are…

newest_articles.title: Stronger and more frequent hurricanes threaten job growth in coastal counties
newest_articles.excerpt: The southeastern coast of the United States is threatened yearly by hurricanes, some receiving more media coverage and resources than others. Coastal states such as Florida experience more hurricane impacts than other states and thus the greatest labor market disruptions from these disasters. My recent study analyzes…

central_articles.title: Seasonally and weather-adjusting the monthly jobs numbers
central_articles.excerpt: april jobs numbers The Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) employment report, released on May 3, 2019, shows that 263,000 jobs were gained in April 2019. Each month, I report results from three alternative projections, each of which was calculated using methodology outlined in my past research published in the Brookings…

central_articles.title: January employment growth boosted by mild weather
central_articles.excerpt: The Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) employment report released last Friday shows that 227,000 new jobs were added in January 2017. central_articles.title: Alternative seasonal adjustments show even stronger jobs growth than the BLS official number
central_articles.excerpt: The Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) employment report released today shows that 313,000 jobs were gained in February.
